prospect.Summary: A high-waisted, bubble-butted southpaw with a rectangular frame, Casilla was one of the more exciting pop-up arms of early 2024 backfield action, as the teenage lefty's fastball was routinely in the 95-98 mph range, and he flashed a nasty two-planed breaking ball. Frustratingly hittable and wild, he posted a 32.9% K% and 16.2% BB% in 12 regular season appearances, then Casilla seemed to get hurt during Instructional League activity toward the end of September. The exact severity of the injury is unclear, but it looked pretty bad. His fastball angle and shape cause it to play beneath its velocity, his command dilutes the effectiveness of his entire repertoire, and he needs to develop a third pitch in order to start, but Casilla's arm strength and breaking ball quality are a great starting point for a young pitcher. If his injury was bad enough to cost him a portion of 2025, it will funnel him further toward a reliever future, though he has late-inning upside in that role.
